response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but the profile does not match this role's core mandate. The job requires 5–10 years of hands-on C++ development on Windows plus SQL Server/Oracle and capital-markets or fixed-income domain expertise. The candidate's C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project, with the bulk of their career in D, TypeScript/Node.js, and web platforms. Financial-domain exposure is limited to a year at a German investment-platform startup using a Node/GraphQL stack, with no fixed-income, derivatives, or investment-banking systems work. Strengths in testing culture and CI/CD do not offset the missing mandatory skills.
strengths: Broad senior-level engineering experience across web, backend, and low-latency systems (including high-throughput services in D)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community teaching
Some fintech exposure at RIDE Capital and experience at top-tier companies (Google, Meta)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014–2015), far short of the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking systems experience
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and craftsmanship background
